guest: Jesse Saler, entrepreneur and endurance athlete
dynamics: Shaan visits Jesse at his home for a year-planning masterclass. Shaan asks questions and learns Jesse's system; Jesse teaches and guides throughout. High energy with varied segments: home tour, workout, sauna, and planning session. Format emphasizes experiential learning rather than traditional podcast studio setup.
frameworks: Getting Light: clearing physical/digital clutter before new year
Misogi: one big year-defining accomplishment annually
Calendar as autobiography: schedule reflects your true priorities
Year-end review: inventory highlights to see full picture
learnings: Small cleanup actions create momentum that carries into larger commitments
Most people plan life around work; inverting this (work around life) dramatically improves satisfaction
Your calendar reveals your actual priorities better than stated goals
Children absorb your work ethic through observation, not instruction
Physical environment cleanliness correlates with mental readiness
Handwritten communication carries emotional weight that digital messages lack
Having one major accomplishment per year compounds into a remarkable life over decades

banger_quotes.quote: We don't lack time, we lack a system
The calendar is your most honest autobiography
Plan work around your life, not life around work
If you don't have 6 minutes, you don't have a life
That's an ink stain in your brain
You don't get a lot of years
There's no Zoom calls in my book

numbers_dropped: Jesse's son ran a marathon at age 9
Jesse writes 10-25 handwritten thank-you letters yearly
notable: Shaan plays piano for the first time in front of an audience during the episode, fulfilling his misogi goal for the year.
